channel.title: Nicotine Science and Policy
channel.description: Daily digest of views, debates and discussions on nicotine science, policy, regulation and advocacy.

item.title: Where’s the Outrage? | Lack of Urgency Mires U.S. Nicotine Industry | RegWatch
item.description: The lack of urgency in US nicotine regulation is leaving adult smokers with fewer legal, lower-risk options while cigarettes remain widely available. Delays, uncertainty and uneven enforcement weaken the regulated market and create space for illicit products to grow. Harm reduction cannot work if safer alternatives are trapped in regulatory limbo. Public health should move faster to support products that help people leave combustion behind. (Published on June 24, 2026byregulatorwatch.com)

item.title: Secret Emails Reveal Sketchy Tactics in California Public Health's Tobacco Ban 'Endgame'
item.description: Public records from California raise troubling questions about how tobacco “endgame” policies are being advanced. When health agencies and funded partners coordinate messaging, polling and local campaigns behind the scenes, public consultation risks becoming political theatre rather than genuine debate. Tobacco policy should be transparent, evidence-based and accountable to the communities it affects. Public health loses trust when outcomes appear to be managed before the public has been heard. (Published on June 24, 2026byreason.com)
